followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: Added the κ arithmetic inconsistency (η·ρ=0.169 vs stated κ=0.400 in Fig. 2 caption) as a new technical objection—missed in cycle 1. Added noise/SNR as a missing assumption (valid point from Gemini). Made the real-splitting vs complex-splitting conditioning limitation more explicit as a separate objection (sharpened from Codex). Added the residue-ratio bound gap as a separate objection (from Codex, valid). Lowered technical_soundness from 5 to 4 due to the κ inconsistency and real-splitting-only conditioning derivation. Overall score held at 4.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Gemini's framing of the Taylor expansion as fatally 'oversold' is overstated as a technical objection; it is better classified as a low-novelty issue. The paper does not misrepresent the math, it just frames a basic result in elaborate language.

Codex's claim that the κ inconsistency and absent supplemental 'would_change_verdict_if_true' for a verdict upgrade to promising/strong_select is too strong; fixing these issues would at most upgrade routine to a cleaner routine, not to a high-impact result.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Codex: Fig. 2 caption κ=0.400 contradicts κ=η·ρ=0.169 given stated η=0.260, ρ=0.650—a concrete numerical inconsistency I missed in cycle 1.
Codex: Conditioning result is proved only on the real-splitting slice (Eq. 8) but is applied to complex-splitting Kerr QNMs without derivation.
Codex: The η² residual hierarchy depends on residue ratios beyond η alone; explicit bounds are missing.
Codex: Kerr realization parameters (mode pair, T_eff, protocol) are too under-specified to reproduce.
Gemini: Noise and SNR are entirely absent. The ill-conditioning advantage is SNR-dependent and this omission leaves the practical claims incomplete.
Gemini: Key Kerr section terms ('L-only band', 'surrogate layer', 'validation band') are undefined and irreproducible.

main_claims.claim: The resolved simple-pole basis becomes ill-conditioned on a finite window as pole splitting δ→0, with Gram matrix condition number diverging.
main_claims.notes: Correct classical result (Prony/Vandermonde near-degeneracy). Numerical Fig. 1 is consistent. The explicit Gram entry formula in Eq. (8) is derived only along the real-splitting slice, not the complex-splitting case relevant to Kerr QNMs.
main_claims.paper_location: Section 'Finite-window necessity of centered organization', Eq. (8), Fig. 1

main_claims.claim: The centered first-jet basis {e^{iωct}, t·e^{iωct}} remains O(1)-conditioned on the finite window as δ→0.
main_claims.notes: The two basis functions are linearly independent with non-vanishing Gram determinant for any finite T regardless of δ. The constant 7+4√3 for the centered basis condition number is stated without derivation in the main text; proof is deferred to absent Supplemental Sec. C.
main_claims.paper_location: Section 'Finite-window necessity of centered organization', Eq. (8), Fig. 1

main_claims.claim: An exact algebraic reorganization of the two-pole Green-function block (Eq. 3) exists around center ωc and half-splitting δ, without requiring a true pole merger.
main_claims.notes: Straightforward partial fraction algebra around (ω-ωc)²-δ². The actual formula content is invisible in the HTML due to rendering failure, but the reorganization is algebraically uncontroversial.

main_claims.claim: The time-domain waveform from a near-degenerate pair has a carrier-plus-first-jet structure at leading order in ε=|δ|T, with next-order error controlled by η=|δ|²T².
main_claims.notes: Follows from Taylor-expanding e^{iδt} ≈ 1+iδt+O(δ²t²). The zeroth-order error in Fig. 3 does not collapse cleanly onto a single ε curve, which the paper acknowledges. Additionally, there is a concrete arithmetic inconsistency in Fig. 2 caption: with stated η=0.260 and ρ=0.650, the product η·ρ=0.169, not κ=0.400 as reported, contradicting Eq. (13) which defines κ=η·ρ.

main_claims.claim: Kerr QNMs in a near-degenerate neighboring-overtone sector show the same conditioning advantage of the centered basis, with amplification ratio of order several at representative small-δ points.
main_claims.notes: Analysis restricted to conditioning comparisons only. Key terms ('L-only band', 'surrogate layer', 'validation band') are undefined. The mode pair, T_eff, perturbation/noise model, surrogate construction, and validation criterion are not specified. Not reproducible from the text.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Noise and SNR are entirely absent from the analysis. Ill-conditioning of the resolved basis is only practically limiting when measurement noise is present; in a noiseless setting one can invert ill-conditioned systems to machine precision.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Throughout, but especially 'Finite-window necessity of centered organization'
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The practical resolving power on a finite window depends on both |δ|T and SNR jointly. The centered-basis advantage over the resolved basis is SNR-dependent; without this, the paper's practical claims are incomplete.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Neighboring-pole dominance: the two-pole block dominates the full Green-function response on the restricted window, with all other poles and branch-cut contributions negligible.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: For real Kerr ringdowns, multiple overtones and branch-cut contributions are present. The validity of the local two-pole approximation is not quantified.

missing_assumptions.assumption: ε=|δ|T is assumed small, but no criterion is given for when relevant Kerr QNM near-degenerate pairs satisfy ε≪1 at astrophysically accessible spin values and observation windows.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If ε is not small for the relevant pairs, the entire expansion is inapplicable to real data.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The residues R₁, R₂ are of comparable magnitude with no anomalous cancellation in A=R₁+R₂. The paper carves out the suppressed-A case without characterizing its frequency for Kerr QNMs.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Specific initial data may preferentially excite one mode, making A small and invalidating the zeroth-order approximation without warning.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The real-splitting conditioning analysis (Eq. 8) extends to complex splittings without modification.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Kerr QNM splittings are complex; the Gram matrix structure and condition number for complex σ on a finite window may differ from the real-slice result.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: In the PDF, does Eq. (13) define κ=ηρ, and if so are the Fig. 2 caption values η=0.260, ρ=0.650, κ=0.400 consistent? If κ≠ηρ the definition or the numerics contain an error.
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: All equation content is blank in the HTML bundle. The arithmetic check requires seeing the actual formula and caption values from the PDF.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the Supplemental Material (Secs. A, B, C) contain valid derivations for the Green-function reduction, Gram matrix condition-number formula (7+4√3), and coefficient-recovery tests?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplemental is absent from the bundle. The core conditioning claims depend on results proven only there.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: What specific Kerr spin values, QNM mode-pair labels (n, l, m), and observation window T_eff were used in the Kerr realization? Are the quoted amplification ratios reproducible from publicly available QNM tables?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The HTML renders blank where numerical values appear, and the protocol for the Kerr inverse problem is not specified in the main text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: How does the centered-basis approach compare quantitatively to SVD-regularized Prony or ESPRIT for near-degenerate mode extraction in terms of parameter recovery error at finite SNR?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: No such comparison is made in the paper; assessing practical advance over the signal-processing literature requires domain expertise and benchmarking outside the bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the real-splitting Gram matrix conditioning analysis (Eq. 8) extended to complex splittings in the supplemental, and if so does the conclusion hold for the damping-rate difference relevant to Kerr overtone pairs?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text restricts to real splitting explicitly; the extension would be in the absent supplemental.

novelty_assessment: The core observation—that near-degenerate exponentials are ill-conditioned on finite windows and should be replaced by a center-plus-variation basis—is classical in numerical analysis (Prony's method, Vandermonde conditioning, ESPRIT/MUSIC literature). The paper's specific contribution is (a) casting this in the frequency-domain Green-function language of GW ringdown, (b) connecting it to Jordan-chain/exceptional-point terminology, and (c) defining the two-scale (ε, η) hierarchy for the two-pole case. These are potentially useful reframings for the GW ringdown community. However, the paper does not compare against existing near-degenerate mode extraction methods, does not cite the relevant signal-processing prior work, and the 'two-scale hierarchy' is essentially a two-term Taylor expansion with named parameters. Novelty is incremental at best.
score_rationale: The central mathematical observation (ill-conditioning of near-degenerate exponential bases on finite windows; carrier-plus-first-jet expansion) is correct but classical. The concrete κ arithmetic inconsistency in Fig. 2 (η·ρ=0.169 vs stated κ=0.400) is a quality-control concern that was missed in cycle 1. The conditioning derivation is restricted to the real-splitting slice and not extended to complex-splitting Kerr QNMs. The Kerr section uses undefined terminology and is not reproducible. The absent supplemental material prevents full verification. No comparison with established near-degenerate mode-extraction methods is made. Noise/SNR is entirely absent from the analysis, making practical claims incomplete. Technical soundness drops to 4 due to the κ inconsistency and real-vs-complex-splitting gap. Novelty remains 4 (incremental reframing). Significance 5 (potentially useful to GW ringdown community as framing). Clarity 3 (equation rendering failure, opaque Kerr section). Overall 4: competent but routine, with unresolved numerical and methodological issues.
summary: The paper argues that near-degenerate resonance poles on a finite observation window are better represented in a centered basis (carrier plus first-jet) rather than as two independently resolved damped sinusoids. The resolved basis becomes ill-conditioned as the pole splitting δ→0, while the centered first-jet basis remains well-conditioned. Two diagnostics ε~|δ|T and η~|δ|²T² define a two-scale hierarchy, verified with toy numerics and a representative Kerr QNM sector. The paper is a methods-level contribution aimed at the GW ringdown spectroscopy community.

technical_objections.evidence: Codex reviewer flagged this at 0.99 confidence. If κ=ηρ by definition, the three stated values η=0.260, ρ=0.650, κ=0.400 are mutually inconsistent. This is either a definition ambiguity, a typo, or an arithmetic error in the numerical demonstration.
technical_objections.objection: Arithmetic inconsistency in Fig. 2 parameters: κ=0.400 is reported but η·ρ = 0.260×0.650 = 0.169 ≠ 0.400, contradicting Eq. (13) which defines κ=ηρ.

technical_objections.evidence: The source HTML shows equations as rows of empty table cells. The bundle itself notes 'Experimental support, please view the build logs for errors.'
technical_objections.objection: All equation content is absent from the submitted bundle due to HTML rendering failures; Eqs. (1)-(15) are present only as blank table scaffolding.

technical_objections.evidence: At least five distinct claims are deferred to the Supplemental with phrases like 'see Sec. C of the Supplemental Material.' The main text is not self-contained without them.
technical_objections.objection: The Supplemental Material (Secs. A, B, C), cited for the Green-function derivation, basis constructions, Gram matrix condition numbers, and coefficient-recovery tests, is entirely absent from the bundle.
technical_objections.paper_location: Sections 'Local effective two-pole response skeleton', 'Finite-window necessity', 'Local interference diagnostics'

technical_objections.evidence: Paper explicitly states Eq. (8) is 'along the transparent real-splitting slice.' Kerr QNM splittings are complex. The extension to complex splittings is asserted but not derived in the main text or cited supplemental.
technical_objections.objection: The conditioning result (Eq. 8) is derived only along the real-splitting slice, but is applied rhetorically to complex-splitting Kerr QNMs without the corresponding complex-window analysis.

technical_objections.evidence: Codex reviewer identified this at 0.78 confidence. Expanding Eq. (10), relative error at next order depends on the ratio ρ=|(R₊-R₋)/(R₊+R₋)| and on normalization, not only on η².
technical_objections.objection: The η² residual hierarchy after first-jet correction is not derived with explicit residue bounds; the remainder from Eq. (10) includes terms proportional to (R₊+R₋)δ²t² and (R₊-R₋)δ³t³, so the scaling depends on residue ratios beyond η alone.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper does not identify the QNM pair labels, T_eff, perturbation/noise model, or inverse problem definition. Data available only by request.
technical_objections.objection: The Kerr realization section uses undefined terminology ('L-only band', 'surrogate layer', 'validation band') and provides insufficient detail to reproduce the results.

technical_objections.evidence: References [25,26] cited for 'finite-time windowing and resolvability considerations' are GW-specific (Talbot 2021, Clarke 2024), not the numerical analysis or signal-processing literature where this result is classical.
technical_objections.objection: The paper does not engage with the signal-processing literature on near-degenerate exponential fitting (Prony, ESPRIT, MUSIC, SVD-regularized methods), making novelty impossible to assess rigorously.

technical_objections.evidence: Paper acknowledges 'remaining spread' and appeals to two-scale fits involving both ε and η to improve the description.
technical_objections.objection: The zeroth-order error in Fig. 3 does not collapse onto a single ε curve, weakening the claim that ε alone controls correction onset.
